title: Jacea cineraria
intro: Jacea cineraria, also known as burnet, is a perennial herb belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Jacea cineraria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Jacea cineraria is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and salves, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Jacea cineraria is classified as a species within the genus Jacea, which is a part of the family Asteraceae. The family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family, is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. This classification indicates that Jacea cineraria shares characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other members of the Asteraceae family. The genus Jacea is also closely related to other genera within the same family, such as Senecio and Bellis. The specific classification of Jacea cineraria reflects its unique combination of morphological and anatomical features.
